prompt:
Ticket: inventory service MVP for the warehouse tooling. Small JSON REST server, in-memory storage for now but storage WILL move to Postgres next quarter, so the HTTP layer must be written against a storage interface, not a concrete map. Module go-inventory-api, package inventory.

Exports the tests (and later main) wire up:
- NewServer(store Store) http.Handler
- type Store interface with exactly these methods (context-aware, versioned CAS):
    List(ctx) ([]Item, error)
    Get(ctx, sku) (Item, error)
    Create(ctx, it Item) (Item, error)            // assigns Version 1
    Update(ctx, it Item, expectVersion int) (Item, error)  // CAS: bumps Version
    Delete(ctx, sku string, expectVersion int) error
  plus sentinel errors ErrNotFound / ErrExists / ErrVersionMismatch and NewMemStore() giving a race-safe in-memory Store.
- type Item struct { SKU, Name string; Quantity, PriceCents, Version int } — JSON is sku/name/quantity/price_cents; the version must NOT appear in bodies, it travels as a strong ETag: version 3 -> ETag "3".

Endpoints:
- POST /items — create. Validation: sku matches [A-Z0-9][A-Z0-9-]{2,31}, name non-blank, quantity and price_cents >= 0, unknown JSON fields rejected, malformed JSON rejected (all 400 with a JSON error). Duplicate sku -> 409. Success: 201, Location /items/<sku>, ETag "1".
- GET /items — every item, sorted by sku ascending. Empty store -> [] (never null).
- GET /items/{sku} — 200 + ETag; honors If-None-Match: matching ETag -> 304, empty body, ETag still set. Unknown -> 404.
- PUT /items/{sku} — full replace, optimistic concurrency. Decision order is fixed: unknown sku -> 404; no If-Match header -> 428; If-Match that doesn't match the current version (stale, garbage, whatever) -> 412 BEFORE the body is even validated; then body validation -> 400 (body sku, when present, must equal the path sku; sku may be omitted); success -> 200 + bumped ETag. If-Match: * matches any current version. Failed writes never bump the version.
- DELETE /items/{sku} — same 404/428/412 ladder, success 204 with empty body. Re-creating a deleted sku starts over at version 1.
- Wrong method on either path -> 405 (status only, stock mux answer is fine).

Every error body is {"error":"..."} with Content-Type application/json, and storage-layer failures surface as 500 WITHOUT leaking the internal error string. Handlers run concurrently; when 20 PUTs race with the same If-Match exactly one may win (the CAS belongs in the store, not read-then-write in the handler).

Acceptance tests: api_test.go and concurrent_test.go — protected, don't touch them. Verify: go test -race -timeout 30s ./... . Split the code sensibly (store/memstore/http handlers/validation in separate files) — I'm going to review this like the production service it's about to become.
